name: Northern Star Credit Union, Inc.
description_short: Federally insured state-chartered credit union headquartered in Portsmouth, VA, serving 7,450 members with $73.5M in assets since 1929.
description_long: Northern Star Credit Union, Inc. is a long-established financial cooperative founded in 1929, operating for 97 years as a federally insured state-chartered credit union. Headquartered in Portsmouth, Virginia, the institution serves approximately 7,450 members across a single branch location with a staff of 18 full and part-time employees. The credit union offers core consumer banking products and services including checking and savings accounts, ATM/debit card programs, and lending services. Their loan portfolio reflects a balanced mix of real estate loans ($11.2M), unsecured personal loans ($16.7M), and other loan categories ($7.4M), demonstrating active lending to membership. The institution maintains $73.5M in total assets with $63.6M in member shares and deposits, positioning it as a mid-sized regional credit union. Northern Star operates as a membership-based credit union with state-chartered natural person membership eligibility. The organization is FHLB (Federal Home Loan Bank) member and maintains federal insurance through NCUSIF. Leadership includes CEO Jennifer Riley and Chairperson Charles R Parker. The credit union generated $1.025M in net income as of its last audit on June 1, 2024, indicating stable operations. As a traditional credit union, Northern Star serves a specific geographic and membership base in the Hampton Roads region of Virginia. The institution's 97-year operating history and conservative balance sheet suggest stability, though members should note the single branch location may limit physical access compared to larger banking networks. The modest asset base and member count position this as a community-focused institution rather than a full-service financial megabank.
pros: 97-year operating history demonstrates long-term stability and community commitment since 1929
Federally insured through NCUSIF with state charter and FHLB membership for regulatory protection
Balanced loan portfolio ($35.4M total loans) serving real estate, personal, and other lending needs
Positive net income of $1.025M with healthy equity position ($9.84M) and low credit loss provisions
Membership-based structure with member-owned governance model led by CEO Jennifer Riley
Modest operating expenses ($1.23M) relative to asset base suggests efficient operations
cons: Single branch location in Portsmouth limits physical access for members outside immediate area
Modest $73.5M asset base and 7,450-member base means fewer technological or service resources than large banks
No investment portfolio ($0 in total investments) limits wealth management and advisory services
Limited public information on loan rates, fees, or online/mobile banking capabilities from available data
Small staff (18 employees) may limit service hours, specialized expertise, or customer support availability
best_for: Portsmouth and Hampton Roads area residents seeking local, community-focused banking with member ownership
Credit union members eligible for state-chartered natural person membership seeking basic checking and savings
Borrowers needing personal, real estate, or unsecured loans from a cooperative institution
diagnosis: Northern Star Credit Union is best for residents in the Portsmouth and Hampton Roads, Virginia area seeking straightforward banking services from a member-owned, federally insured institution with deep community roots. The primary limitation is its single branch location and modest scale, which may restrict service availability and technological capabilities compared to larger regional or national banks.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
